reasoning:
As of early August 2025, Bitcoin is trading in the $115,000 to $118,000 range, establishing a roughly 23-26% margin above the target threshold of $93,429.20. With about 5 months left in the year, Bitcoin only needs to avoid a net >20% decline by the end of December to resolve YES. A standard options/asset-pricing model incorporating Bitcoin's historically high volatility (~50-60%) yields an implied probability of about 65% to 75% for it to remain above $93.4K. 

The community prediction in late July was 62%, which may have factored in the risk of macroeconomic headwinds, potential late-year corrections, or simply long-tailed downside volatility that crypto markets often exhibit. However, given strong recent monthly closes and the fact that 5 months is a sufficiently short time horizon to benefit from such a solid head start, an upward update to 0.68 is appropriate. It recognizes the tangible mathematical advantage of being 25% "in the money" while still respecting Bitcoin's intrinsic capacity for large swings and sharp drawdowns.
